Discovery is the work of figuring out what to build.

Product teams often conflate discovery with delivery, but the two disciplines require different mindsets. During discovery, we leverage prototypes and rapid customer interviews to de-risk assumptions before writing any production code.

A strong product manager will frame outcomes rather than outputs. They champion the user, push back on vanity features, and align stakeholders around evidence rather than opinion.

Roadmaps should be treated as living artifacts. When new evidence surfaces, the team revisits priorities and reallocates capacity to the highest-leverage opportunities.
